channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: We got a sneak peek at the Asus ROG Phone 9, one of the first Snapdragon 8 Elite phones, and it's going to be fast
item.description: Several Android manufacturers have confirmed that their upcoming devices will be powered by the Snapdragon 8 Elite chipset.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Two new HHS programs to help with complicated health challenges
item.description: "A lot of the efforts that we're doing across the government have a plug into the artificial intelligence world," said Andy Kilianski. The post Two new HHS programs to help with complicated health challenges first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: This viral Tim Walz content was created by Russia, U.S. intelligence confirms
item.description: Groups in Russia created and helped spread viral disinformation targeting Democratic vice presidential candidate Tim Walz, a senior U.S. intelligence official said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Alkira expands NaaS platform with ZTNA capabilities
item.description: Alkira is expanding its namesake network-as-a-service (NaaS) platform with new zero trust network access (ZTNA) capabilities. Alkira provides a SaaS service for on-demand network infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: As coal plants shut in Romania, some miners transition to green energy while others are reluctant
item.description: As Romania prepares to phase out coal by 2032, some miners are retraining for jobs in renewable energies.
item.dc:creator: Anca Gurzu, Cipher News, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Los CIO deben convertirse en evangelizadores de las TI
item.description: Los evangelizadores de la tecnología abundan en el sector de las TI, pero normalmente el término se utiliza para describir una función relacionada con el marketing destinada a promocionar un producto, servicio o tecnología.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Anthropic debuts AI agents that save coders extra keystrokes
item.description: Anthropic, a startup backed by Alphabet and Amazon.com, released a pair of updated artificial intelligence models on Tuesday, along with a new capability to autonomously perform computer tasks and save users keystrokes.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: What a Trump win could mean for Elon Musk's businesses
item.description: Elon Musk is wearing multiple hats this election season, sparking questions about how his dual role as a tech leader and vocal surrogate for former President Trump could lead to conflicts of interest.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Big offshore wind project proposed for New York as other sites are evaluated in 3 states
item.description: BRIGANTINE, N.J. (AP) — Offshore wind energy projects in New York, New Jersey and Maryland are moving forward, as federal regulators examine the proposals and opponents escalate their legal challenges to the work.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The iPad mini is still the best small tablet around
item.description: We waited three years for Apple to update the iPad mini, and what we got is more of the same — boosted with a much better processor. Apple’s iPad mini has consistently been positioned as either the most versatile, or the most underpowered tablet in the company’s lineup.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Giant fish thought to be extinct is spotted in the Mekong River
item.description: Scientists say a giant fish in the Mekong River thought to be extinct has been spotted three times in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tech giants press Congress to codify AI Safety Institute
item.description: A coalition of more than 60 tech companies and industry groups, including Amazon, Anthropic, Google, Meta, Microsoft and OpenAI, urged congressional leaders Tuesday to pass legislation permanently authorizing the U.S. Artificial Intelligence Safety Institute by the end of the year.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Elon Musk says he’ll fix the government under Trump. His track record paints a different picture
item.description: Elon Musk is making some big promises while campaigning for former President Donald Trump. But his business record calls into question whether he can deliver.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com, The Mercury News

item.title: 2024 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: FTC's rule banning fake online reviews goes into effect
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal rule banning fake online reviews is now in effect. The Federal Trade Commission issued the rule in August banning the sale or purchase of online reviews.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NYC Department of Education builds the pipeline for future cybersecurity professionals
item.description: There’s been a lot of talk over the past few years about a shortage of skilled cybersecurity talent, but from where we sit at the NYC DOE Division of Information and Instructional Technology (DIIT), we see budding security pros all around us.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: State Department making sure change is more than just a name
item.description: Kelly Fletcher, the State Department’s CIO, said the newly named Bureau of Diplomatic Technology is aligning cybersecurity and customer experience. The post State Department making sure change is more than just a name first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Cuban says Harris campaign rejected live X interview with Musk
item.description: Billionaire investor Mark Cuban said Monday that Vice President Harris’s campaign rejected a live interview on the social platform X with billionaire Elon Musk, who owns the platform.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Biden administration to provide $325 million for new Michigan semiconductor factory
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Monday that it would provide up to $325 million to Hemlock Semiconductor for a new factory, a move that could help give Democrats a political edge in the swing state of Michigan ahead of election day.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Microsoft to launch autonomous AI agents in November
item.description: Microsoft will soon let customers build autonomous AI agents that can be configured to perform complex tasks with little or no input from humans.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Narrowing the Federal Cybersecurity Skills Gap: A Multifaceted Approach
item.description: Federal agencies must prioritize upskilling opportunities, diversity, and AI tools to stay ahead of evolving cyber threats
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Army aims for speed in new $10B software contract
item.description: Potential bidders have until the end of this week to offer comments on the Army's latest draft request for proposals. 15-20 firms will win spots on the contract The post Army aims for speed in new $10B software contract first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jared Serbu, Federal News Network

item.title: Fake IT workers from North Korea have started blackmailing their victims
item.description: A new report from Secureworks shows that the North Korean group Nickel Tapestry has expanded its operations from getting North Korean IT workers illegally employed by companies in other countries to allowing the workers to steal data that can be used for extortion if they’re fired, according to The
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Buyer’s guide: How to choose between Microsoft 365 and Office 2024
item.description: Microsoft Office — best known for its Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Outlook productivity applications — is how billions of people around the world work and study, whether they do it from home, an office, a classroom, or a combination of those.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Buyer’s guide: Edge data centers
item.description: Edge data centers include hardware, software, applications, data management, connectivity, gateways, security, and advanced analytics. They come in all shapes and sizes depending on the use case: Small ones can fit into a wiring closet. Larger ones resemble traditional data centers.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Over 600M cyberattacks target Windows users every day
item.description: Windows operating system users face more than 600 million cyberattacks every day, according to Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson. The attacks are on the rise.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
